title: Speaking: Where Are You From?
learningGoal: Engage in a fluid conversation stating your name, country, and asking the same of someone else.
instructions: You will now roleplay a conversation. You will meet Anjali. Introduce yourself by name, ask her where she is from, and state where you are from. Speak clearly and remember the SOV word order.
speakingPrompt: Respond to Anjali using complete Hindi sentences.
scenario: Meeting a new person and exchanging origins.

subTasks.prompt: Anjali says: 'Namaste. Main Anjali hoon.' (Hello. I am Anjali.) Reply with: 'Hello. I am John.'
Now, ask her formally: 'Where are you from?'
Anjali says: 'Main Bharat se hoon. Aur aap?' (I am from India. And you?). Reply: 'I am from America.'
Anjali points to your friend Peter and asks: 'Aur yeh kaun hai?' (And who is this?). Reply: 'This is Peter.'

scenarioContext.targetText: आप अंजलि से बात कर रहे हैं।
scenarioContext.transliteration: Aap Anjali se baat kar rahe hain.

acceptableParaphrases: Using your real name and country is perfectly acceptable.

criteria.name: Verb Matching
criteria.description: Used 'hoon' for I, 'hain' for You, and 'hai' for Third Person.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Namaste. Main John hoon. Aap kahan se hain? Main Amerika se hoon. Yeh Peter hai.

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic! You are now capable of introducing yourself and a friend in Hindi.
feedbackRules.onFail: Make sure you use the correct verb for each person. Main = hoon, Aap = hain, Yeh = hai.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Practice saying 'Aap kahan se hain' faster so it sounds natural.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: नमस्ते। मैं जॉन हूँ। आप कहाँ से हैं? मैं अमेरिका से हूँ। यह पीटर है।

hintText: Speak confidently. Remember the 'se' goes right after the country name.
commonMistakes: Saying 'Main hoon se Amerika' (incorrect word order).
